source_section.title_label_raw: Built Form and Siting Requirements within the CEN- 2 and CEN-1 Zones Applicability

sections.title_label_raw: Siting Requirements within the CEN- 2 and CEN-1 Zones Applicability

provisions.heading_context_raw: Siting Requirements within the CEN- 2 and CEN-1 Zones Applicability
provisions.text: With the exception of main buildings within a heritage conservation district, any main building erected, constructed, reconstructed, altered, or located, or an addition to any main building, within a CEN- 2 or CEN-1 zone shall meet the built form and siting requirements of this Chapter. Maximum Building Height

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Sections 103 and 104, and Part X, any main building shall

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Sections 103 and 104, and Part X, any main building shall
provisions.text: Subject to Sections 103 and 104, and Part X, any main building shall not exceed the maximum required building height specified on Schedule 15. Maximum Floor Area Ratio (FAR)

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 158(3), any main building's floor area

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 158(3), any main building's floor area
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 158(3), any main building's floor area shall not exceed the maximum FAR value as specified on Schedule 17.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 158(3), any main building's floor area
provisions.text: Where there are multiple FAR precincts on a lot, only the area of the lot that is covered by a FAR precinct shall be used to complete the calculation within that FAR precinct.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 158(3), any main building's floor area
provisions.text: Where a portion of a lot has been acquired by the Province of Nova Scotia, the Halifax Dartmouth Bridge Commission, or the Municipality for the purpose of street widening after the coming into force date of this By-law, and a FAR value is shown on Schedule 17 for the subject property, any reduction in the lot area shall not affect the amount of floor area that would have been achievable on the coming into force date of this By-law.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 158(3), any main building's floor area
provisions.text: If no maximum required FAR value is specified on Schedule 17, no maximum required FAR value applies. Minimum Front or Flanking Setbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 159(2), any portion of a main building,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 159(2), any portion of a main building,
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 159(2), any portion of a main building, either above or below grade, shall have a minimum required front or flanking setback as specified on Schedule 18. (RCCC- Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 159(2), any portion of a main building,
provisions.text: If a minimum required front or flanking setback has not been specified on Schedule 18, the minimum required front or flanking setback shall be 1.5 metres. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22) Maximum Front or Flanking Setbacks

sections.title_label_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements for any main building located:

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: on a registered heritage property; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: on a lot containing a community recreation use.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: Repealed (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: Subject to Section 161, any main building located along a pedestrian-oriented commercial street, as shown on Schedule 7, shall have a maximum front or flanking setback, as specified on Schedule 19, unless Subsections 160(3), 160(4), or 160(5) apply.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: Maximum front or flanking setbacks shall not apply to portions of lots that have a frontage less than 4.0 metres along a streetline or transportation reserve.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: Where a transportation or utility easement abuts the streetline, a main building shall have a maximum front or flanking setback that is the greater of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: the applicable distance as specified on Schedule 19; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: the edge of the easement that is located farthest from the streetline.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: The maximum front or flanking setback requirement of Subsection 160(2) may be exceeded, if an at-grade open space, such as a plaza, is located between a front or flanking lot line and a main building.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 160(7) and 161(3), if a maximum front or flanking setback is not specified on Schedule 19, the maximum required front or flanking setback shall be 4.0 metres. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: The maximum front or flanking setback may be exceeded, providing that:

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: the setback is not exceeded by more than 50% of the façade; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: the requirements in subsection 420(7) are met. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24) Maximum Front or Flanking Setback Exemption

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 160(3), 160(4), 160(5), and 161(2), no

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 160(3), 160(4), 160(5), and 161(2), no
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 160(3), 160(4), 160(5), and 161(2), no more than 30% of the width of the streetwall may exceed the maximum front or flanking setback requirement of Section 160.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 160(3), 160(4), 160(5), and 161(2), no
provisions.text: Where a development makes use of both Subsection 161(1) and Subsection 167(1), the combined streetwall exemptions shall not exceed 30% of the entire width of the streetwall.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 160(3), 160(4), 160(5), and 161(2), no
provisions.text: Subsection 160(6) shall not apply to 20 Best Street, Dartmouth (PID 40534752). (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24) Side Setback Requirements

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 162(2) and 162(3), and Section 427, the

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 162(2) and 162(3), and Section 427, the
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 162(2) and 162(3), and Section 427, the minimum required side setback for any main building shall be:

provisions.text: where a lot line abuts a lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone, 6.0 metres from the side lot line abutting the lot; or (RCCC- Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 162(2) and 162(3), and Section 427, the
provisions.text: 0.0 metre elsewhere.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 162(2) and 162(3), and Section 427, the
provisions.text: Underground parking structures are not required to have a minimum side setback, providing they do not protrude more than 0.6 metre above the average finished grade in any side yard.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 162(2) and 162(3), and Section 427, the
provisions.text: For a high-rise building, any portion of the main building above the streetwall height shall have a minimum required setback from any side lot line of:

provisions.text: 6.0 metres, if the abutting property is within a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, UC- 2, or UC-1 zone and has a view plane restriction that would not permit the development of a high-rise building; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 162(2) and 162(3), and Section 427, the
provisions.text: 12.5 metres elsewhere.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 162(2) and 162(3), and Section 427, the
provisions.text: There is no maximum required side setback. Rear Setback Requirements

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 163(2), 163(3), and 163(5), and Section

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 163(2), 163(3), and 163(5), and Section
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 163(2), 163(3), and 163(5), and Section 427, the minimum required rear setback for any main building shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 163(2), 163(3), and 163(5), and Section
provisions.text: where a lot line abuts a lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone, 6.0 metres from the rear lot line abutting the lot; (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 163(2), 163(3), and 163(5), and Section
provisions.text: where a lot line abuts a lot, any portion of which, is zoned COR, HR-2, HR-1, CLI, LI, HRI, INS, UC-2, UC-1, DND, H, CDD-2, or CDD-1 zone, 3.0 metres from the rear lot line abutting the lot, except as provided in Clause 163(1)(a); or (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 163(2), 163(3), and 163(5), and Section
provisions.text: 0.0 metre elsewhere.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 163(2), 163(3), and 163(5), and Section
provisions.text: Underground parking structures are not required to have a minimum rear setback, providing they do not protrude more than 0.6 metre above the average finished grade in any rear yard.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 163(2), 163(3), and 163(5), and Section
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 163(4), for a tall mid-rise building, any portion of the main building exceeding a height of 7 storeys shall have a minimum required setback of 4.5 metres from a rear lot line. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 163(2), 163(3), and 163(5), and Section
provisions.text: The requirement of Subsection 163(3) shall be reduced to zero where abutting lots are under common ownership and developed over a continuous foundation, footing, or underground parking structure.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 163(2), 163(3), and 163(5), and Section
provisions.text: For a high-rise building, any portion of the main building above the streetwall height shall have a minimum required setback from any rear lot line of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 163(2), 163(3), and 163(5), and Section
provisions.text: 6.0 metres, if the abutting property is within a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, UC- 2, or UC-1 zone and has a view plane restriction that would not permit the development of a high-rise building; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 163(2), 163(3), and 163(5), and Section
provisions.text: 12.5 metres elsewhere. Maximum Streetwall Heights

sections.title_label_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall
provisions.text: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall be: (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall
provisions.text: on a registered heritage property, the streetwall height of the registered heritage building on the coming into force date of this By-law;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall
provisions.text: for a main building except under Clause 164(1)(a), that is no taller than 5 storeys, the building height; or ((RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall
provisions.text: 3 storeys elsewhere. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall
provisions.text: Repealed (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall
provisions.text: Repealed (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24) Minimum Streetwall Heights

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 165(2) and 165(3), any main building's

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 165(2) and 165(3), any main building's
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 165(2) and 165(3), any main building's minimum required streetwall height shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 165(2) and 165(3), any main building's
provisions.text: along pedestrian-oriented commercial streets, as shown on Schedule 7, 2 storeys; or (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 165(2) and 165(3), any main building's
provisions.text: along all other streets or transportation reserves:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 165(2) and 165(3), any main building's
provisions.text: for any main building that is 2 storeys or less, the building height, or (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 165(2) and 165(3), any main building's
provisions.text: for any main building that is more than 2 storeys, 2 storeys. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 165(2) and 165(3), any main building's
provisions.text: Twenty percent or less of the entire streetwall width, to a maximum of 10.0 metres, may be reduced in height, providing the height is not reduced below 1 storey (Diagram 7). (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 165(2) and 165(3), any main building's
provisions.text: The minimum streetwall height required in Subsection 165(1) may be reduced to 1 storey along a streetline or a transportation reserve, if the slope exceeds 4% across the building width or building depth. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24) Streetwall Stepbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 166(2) and 166(3), any main building

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 166(2) and 166(3), any main building
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 166(2) and 166(3), any main building shall have a minimum required streetwall stepback of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 166(2) and 166(3), any main building
provisions.text: 2.0 metres for mid-rise buildings; (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 166(2) and 166(3), any main building
provisions.text: 3.0 metres for tall mid-rise buildings; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 166(2) and 166(3), any main building
provisions.text: 4.5 metres for high-rise buildings.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 166(2) and 166(3), any main building
provisions.text: No streetwall stepback is required for any main building that meets Clause 164(1)(b) (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22).

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 166(2) and 166(3), any main building
provisions.text: No streetwall stepback is required for a maximum of 20% of the length of any main building facing each streetline or a transportation reserve provided that:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 166(2) and 166(3), any main building
provisions.text: each section not having a streetwall stepback does not exceed 14.0 metres in length along the streetline or transportation reserve; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 166(2) and 166(3), any main building
provisions.text: there is a minimum required separation distance of 14.0 metres along the streetline or transportation reserve between sections not having a streetwall stepback. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 166(2) and 166(3), any main building
provisions.text: No portion of any main building above the streetwall shall project beyond the streetwall towards the front or flanking lot line. Recessed Portions and Cantilevers within Streetwalls

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 167(2) and 167(3), the total combined

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 167(2) and 167(3), the total combined
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 167(2) and 167(3), the total combined width of all cantilevered and recessed portions within any streetwall that have a depth of 2.0 metres or greater shall not exceed 30% of the width of the streetwall.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 167(2) and 167(3), the total combined
provisions.text: Cantilevered and recessed portions described in Subsection 167(1) shall include portals, but exclude balconies and recessed pedestrian entrances.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 167(2) and 167(3), the total combined
provisions.text: Where a development makes use of both Subsections 167(1) and 161(1), the combined streetwall exemptions shall not exceed 30% of the entire width of the streetwall. Maximum Lot Coverage

sections.title_label_raw: No maximum required lot coverage applies.

provisions.heading_context_raw: No maximum required lot coverage applies.
provisions.text: No maximum required lot coverage applies. Ground Floor Requirements

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series Highways, as shown on Schedule 52, a ground floor shall be established along any streetline or transportation reserve. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: On a corner lot, for any streetwall along a streetline or transportation reserve that has an average finished grade that exceeds a slope greater than 8%, extended across the entire width of the streetwall, the requirements for a ground floor do not apply.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: A ground floor shall have a minimum required depth of 3.0 metres, as measured from any exterior wall of any main building that is facing a streetline or a transportation reserve.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 169(6), each ground floor required shall, for no less than 70% of each streetwall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Section 74, commence no lower than 0.6 metre below the streetline grade; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Subsection 169(5), commence no higher than 0.6 metre above the streetline grade.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: In the event of a conflict between the coastal elevation requirements of Section 74 and Clause 169(4)(b), Clause 169(4)(b) shall not apply.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: The ground floor of a grade-related dwelling unit use shall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Section 74, commence no lower than 1.5 metres below the streetline grade; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: commence no higher than 1.5 metres above the streetline grade.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 169(8) and excluding a low-density dwelling use, a grade-related dwelling unit use, or a residential lobby on a floor containing grade-related dwelling unit use, the minimum required ground floor height shall be 3.5 metres. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: The ground floor height required in Subsection 169(7) may be reduced where one of the following conditions applies:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: the ground floor height of an addition is equal to or greater than the ground floor height of the existing main building; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 169(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: for a proposed building on a registered heritage property, the ground floor heights of abutting main buildings along the same streetline are such that the required ground floor height of the proposed main building would be inconsistent with the abutting main buildings. Grade-Oriented Premises

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented premises shall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: excluding 100-Series Highways, as shown on Schedule 52, be located along any streetline; (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E- Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: be required for the full width of the streetwall, excluding:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: only one entrance to internal parking located above or below the ground floor, (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a portal,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: an emergency exit, or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: an entrance to a room dedicated to a solid waste management area; (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: not exceed a maximum width of 24.0 metres for each premise facing a streetline: and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: contain an independent pedestrian entrance for each premise facing a streetline.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: Subsection 170(1) shall not apply to:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a change of use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a low-density dwelling use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a religious institution use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: an emergency services use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a cultural use; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a shared housing with special care use. (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: For any addition to any main building, the requirement of Subsection 170(1) shall only apply to the length of the streetwall addition.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 170(2) and 170(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: Grade-oriented premises shall have a minimum required depth of 3.0 metres, as measured from any exterior wall of any main building that is facing a streetline. Grade-Related Dwelling Units

sections.title_label_raw: Every grade-related dwelling unit shall have a required minimum of

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every grade-related dwelling unit shall have a required minimum of
provisions.text: Every grade-related dwelling unit shall have a required minimum of one exterior pedestrian entrance. Side and Rear Stepbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 172(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 172(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 172(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone, the wall of any main building facing the lot with such zone shall have a required side or rear stepback, as measured from the lowest finished grade on that side of the main building, on the 2nd, 3rd, or 4th storey. (RC- May 23/24;E- June 13/24) (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 172(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: The side or rear stepback in Subsection 172(1) shall be a minimum of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 172(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 0.0 metre for low-rise buildings;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 172(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 2.0 metres for mid-rise buildings; (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 172(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 6.0 metres for tall mid-rise buildings; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 172(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 6.0 metres for high-rise buildings.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 172(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: The side or rear stepback under Subsection 172(1) is not required if an entire main building has a minimum side or rear setback of: (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.text: 8.0 metres for mid-rise buildings; (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 172(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 12.0 metres for tall mid-rise buildings; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 172(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 12.0 metres for high-rise buildings. Minimum Separation Distances

sections.title_label_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main building is permitted on a lot in Subsection 89(3), a minimum required separation distance of 4.0 metres shall be provided between main buildings.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: Where more than one portion of any main building protrudes above grade, a minimum required separation distance of 4.0 metres shall be provided between the above grade portions.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 173(4) and 173(5), above the streetwall height, any portions of the same main building shall be separated by a minimum required distance of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 6.0 metres between a mid-rise typology and another mid- rise typology;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 6.0 metres between a mid-rise typology and a tall mid-rise typology;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 12.5 metres between a mid-rise typology and a high-rise typology;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 12.5 metres between a tall mid-rise typology and another tall mid-rise typology;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 12.5 metres between a tall mid-rise typology and a high- rise typology; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 25.0 metres between a high-rise typology and another high-rise typology. (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: Tower portions of two or more main buildings on the same lot shall be separated by a minimum required distance of 25.0 metres. (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: For the property identified by PID 00136341, the minimum required separation distance between a mid-rise typology and a high-rise typology shall be 4.0 metres (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22).

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: For the Quingate Special Area, above the streetwall height, any portions of the same main building shall be separated by a minimum required distance of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 12.0 metres between portions of the main building up to 30.0 metres in height; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 25.0 metres between portions of the main building greater than 30.0 metres in height. (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22) Maximum Building Dimensions

sections.title_label_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average finished grade or any public building use, any main building shall not exceed:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: a building width of 64.0 metres; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: a building depth of 64.0 metres.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: The tower portion of any high-rise building shall not exceed the following maximum building dimension requirements of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: a building depth of 35.0 metres;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: a building width of 40.0 metres; and (RC- May 23/24;E- June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: an average floor area of 900 square metres per storey as calculated for the tower portion of the building. ((RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: For the purpose of measuring building dimensions in Subsections 174(1) and 174(2), main buildings connected by a pedway shall be measured separately, excluding the pedway. Accessory Structures

sections.title_label_raw: Accessory structures shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory structures shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter
provisions.text: Accessory structures shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter 19.
